title: linum alpinum var. pseudomontanum
intro: Linum alpinum var. pseudomontanum, also known as alpine flax or false mountain flax, is a plant species within the Linum genus that is native to high-altitude regions of Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Linum alpinum var. pseudomontanum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
